r_relatable_problem.primary_problem: Memory is genetic, declines with age, can't be significantly improved

s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: Ancient Greek Memory Strategies (Enhanced by Dominic O'Brien)

headline.primary: Develop a memory so powerful, you're like a human computer!
headline.subheadline: Be amazed as the 8-time World Memory Champion teaches you to: Increase mental recall exponentially, Remember names/faces for months/years, Deliver speeches from memory with no notes

pattern_analysis.curiosity_mechanism: How does 8-time champion do it and can I?

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Memory can become computer-like through ancient Greek techniques anyone can learn
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Ancient proven techniques rather than modern memory hacks

big_idea.statement: The ancient Greeks and Romans had stronger memories than we do today because they used systematic memory strategies. You can develop the same superhuman memory using these ancient techniques enhanced by the 8-time World Memory Champion.
big_idea.supporting_idea: Memory power is not genetic or talent-based - it's systematic technique that anyone can master in 10 minutes per day.

mechanism.name: Ancient Greek Memory Strategies (Enhanced by Dominic O'Brien)
mechanism.primary_mechanism: Systematic memory techniques invented by Greek poet Simonides (500 BC), perfected over 2,000 years, enhanced by modern World Memory Champion

promise.primary_promise: Develop a photographic memory like a human computer - remember names, numbers, speeches, dates, anything you want
promise.promise_specificity: Specific capabilities: memorize deck of cards in 30 seconds, remember any date in history instantly, never forget names/faces

believability_factors: Memorized 54 decks (2,808 cards) viewing each once
Banned from casinos for winning with memory
High school dropout transformed into champion

strongest_proof_elements.text: 8-time World Memory Champion. Memorized 2,808 cards (54 decks) viewing each only once. Guinness World Record holder.
Banned from every casino in UK and several in France, Germany, Czech Republic, and US for winning too much using memory skills
High school dropout, poor student (ranked 19th of 22, teachers said 'terribly slow, tends to dream') → World Memory Champion
Memorized 7,500 Trivial Pursuit questions/answers for 'Challenge the Memory Man' promotion - no one could make him make a mistake
Memorized names of 100 people at dinner party in 20 minutes, recalled perfectly later - wealthy businessman gave him £50,000 ($75K) for Las Vegas

proof_inventory.proof_density: Extremely high - world records, casino bans, teacher report cards, transformation story all support

fssit_candidates.statement: Believe it or not, the ancient Greeks and Romans had stronger memories than we do today. Perhaps what we've gained in efficiency has come at the expense of mental agility. We don't have to work our memories as hard as the Greeks and Romans did.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why memory feels weak - modern conveniences atrophied natural memory capacity that ancients developed

fssit_candidates.statement: You don't have to be a genius to have a great memory! Memory master Dominic O'Brien used to be a terrible student. Teachers said: 'Terribly slow. Must concentrate. Often cannot repeat the question.' Ranked 19th out of 22. Yet he became World Memory Champion.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Validates that poor memory/school performance isn't fixed genetics - it's lack of systematic technique

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Memory greatness isn't talent or genetics - it's ancient systematic techniques anyone can learn
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Ancient proven methodology rather than modern memory tricks

narrative_reorganization.self_blame_removal: Poor memory isn't your fault or genetic limitation - you simply haven't learned the ancient systematic techniques
narrative_reorganization.coherence_creation: All those memory problems (forgetting names, numbers, tasks) aren't signs of declining mind - they're signs of untrained memory in modern environment that doesn't require it
narrative_reorganization.new_self_concept: Not someone with poor memory genetics, but someone with dormant memory capacity waiting for systematic training

three_part_structure.what_they_think: Memory is genetic, declines with age, can't be significantly improved
three_part_structure.what_it_really_is: Memory is systematic technique invented by ancient Greeks - modern life atrophied natural capacity that can be rebuilt
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: Previous memory tricks were superficial tactics rather than the systematic ancient methodology that built Greek genius

root_cause_architecture.villain: Modern convenience and belief that memory is fixed genetic trait

notable_patterns: Authority proof is overwhelming (8-time champion, Guinness records)
Casino ban validates real-world profit from skill
Teacher report cards create dramatic before/after
Ancient Greek framing adds historical authority
10 minutes per day makes commitment feel achievable
genius_elements: Report card evidence shows documented transformation
Casino ban proves memory skills = real money
£50,000 dinner party wager creates concrete proof story
Ancient Greek origins add gravitas and proven track record
High school dropout → champion arc proves it's learnable not genetic
